title: Nervilia bicarinata
intro: Nervilia bicarinata, also known as the "tiger orchid," is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, characterized by its unique and striking appearance. The health benefits of Nervilia bicarinata include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit a range of biological activities. Traditionally, Nervilia bicarinata is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat a variety of health conditions, including fever, respiratory infections, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Nervilia bicarinata is classified as a species within the orchid family, specifically belonging to the genus Nervilia. It is a type of terrestrial orchid found in various regions, including Asia and the Pacific Islands. This species is part of the family Orchidaceae and the subfamily Orchidoideae. Nervilia bicarinata is also classified as a member of the tribe Nervilieae. Within the tribe, Nervilia bicarinata is further categorized as a part of the subtribe Nerviliinae.
